skill.name: kubernetes-architect
skill.description: Guides Kubernetes platform architecture, GitOps delivery, multi-cluster operations, security controls, observability, and cost optimization for cloud-native teams.
skill.summary: Plan secure Kubernetes platforms with GitOps, service mesh, observability, and multi-cluster operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography findings are false positives from domain text, such as description, design, desired state, and declarative GitOps wording. The system reconnaissance finding is also a false positive because line 37 is a safety instruction about approvals and rollback plans. No executable code, external commands, network access, secret access, or prompt-injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain Kubernetes architecture prose and GitOps terminology. I found no cryptographic algorithm use, implementation guidance, or insecure crypto recommendation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance, not executable code. The flagged text is ordinary Kubernetes and GitOps language with no crypto API or algorithm reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: Line 37 advises avoiding production changes without approvals and rollback plans. It does not request host inspection, environment discovery, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a safety control statement. There is no command, script, or instruction to enumerate system details.

content.value_statement: Kubernetes platform choices can create security, reliability, and cost risks when teams lack a clear architecture. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan practical platform designs.
actual_capabilities: Designs Kubernetes platform architecture for EKS, AKS, GKE, self-managed clusters, and on-premises environments.
Plans GitOps workflows using ArgoCD, Flux, repository patterns, promotion strategies, and rollback practices.
Recommends security controls for RBAC, Pod Security Standards, network policies, admission control, and supply chain protection.
Maps observability stacks for metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerting in container platforms.
Evaluates multi-tenancy, service mesh, disaster recovery, autoscaling, and cost optimization tradeoffs.
limitations: Does not operate clusters, apply manifests, or make production changes by itself.
Requires current environment details, workload needs, and compliance requirements for precise recommendations.
Does not replace vendor documentation, security review, or change approval processes.
May need human validation for provider-specific limits, pricing, and managed service behavior.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Platform
use_cases.description: Create an architecture for clusters, networking, GitOps, security boundaries, and observability before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Modernize Delivery
use_cases.description: Move from manual releases to GitOps with progressive delivery, environment promotion, and rollback planning.

use_cases.title: Improve Governance
use_cases.description: Review multi-tenancy, RBAC, policies, compliance controls, and supply chain practices for a shared Kubernetes platform.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Cluster Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of an early Kubernetes design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Kubernetes platform plan. Identify missing requirements, major risks, and the next decisions I should make.

prompt_templates.title: Design a GitOps Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a GitOps workflow for these teams and environments. Include repository structure, promotion flow, approval points, and rollback handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Secure Multi-Tenant Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a secure multi-tenant Kubernetes model. Cover namespaces, RBAC, network policies, admission controls, quotas, and tenant onboarding.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Enterprise Platform Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture across clusters and regions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an enterprise Kubernetes platform strategy. Include cluster topology, GitOps, service mesh, observability, disaster recovery, cost controls, and governance.

output_examples.input: Design a Kubernetes platform for three product teams on AWS.
output: A recommended EKS topology with separate staging and production clusters.
A GitOps delivery model with environment promotion and rollback checkpoints.
Security controls for RBAC, network policy, admission checks, and secrets.

output_examples.input: Improve reliability for a multi-cluster application platform.
output: A resilience plan covering autoscaling, multi-region deployment, backups, and recovery targets.
Observability recommendations for metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and alerts.
Operational runbooks for upgrades, incident response, and disaster recovery tests.

output_examples.input: Reduce Kubernetes platform cost without lowering availability.
output: A cost review focused on requests, limits, node utilization, autoscaling, and workload placement.
Savings options using spot capacity, right sizing, and monitoring with Kubecost or OpenCost.
Guardrails to preserve service level objectives during optimization.

best_practices: Provide workload requirements, scale targets, compliance needs, and cloud provider constraints before asking for architecture guidance.
Ask for explicit tradeoffs when comparing GitOps tools, service meshes, tenancy models, or cluster topologies.
Validate recommendations in staging and require rollback plans before production rollout.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for production changes without approval, testing, and recovery steps.
Do not use generic cluster advice when workloads have strict compliance, latency, or availability requirements.
Do not treat the output as a substitute for provider documentation or security review.

faq.question: Can this skill design a complete Kubernetes platform?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline topology, GitOps, security, observability, scaling, resilience, and operating practices.

faq.question: Does it support managed Kubernetes services?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers EKS, AKS, GKE, OpenShift, Rancher, Tanzu, and self-managed clusters.

faq.question: Can it help with GitOps migration?
faq.answer: Yes. It can plan ArgoCD or Flux workflows, repository structures, promotion paths, and rollback strategy.

faq.question: Can it create production-ready manifests?
faq.answer: It can guide manifest structure and controls, but teams must test and review all production changes.

faq.question: Is this useful for security reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review RBAC, Pod Security Standards, network policies, admission controls, secrets, and supply chain practices.

faq.question: What inputs improve the results?
faq.answer: Share cloud provider, workloads, environments, traffic needs, compliance scope, team model, and current platform constraints.
